items.title: Prevent Double Billing on Historical Tasks in Platform Service 🚫
items.summary: This update introduces a safeguard to the billing monitor, ensuring it only bills tasks completed after a designated cutover time. By implementing a `BILLING_CUTOVER` guard, tasks finished before this time will be skipped, preventing any double billing of historical tasks. The changes affect several files, including persister and task store, and include necessary tests to verify the new behavior.

items.title: Enhance DeepSeek Tool Calls with Reasoning Content in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that every tool-calling assistant message in DeepSeek carries non-empty `reasoning_content`, addressing the previous issue of flaky 400 errors during multi-tool calls. The fix involves adding a placeholder for cases without captured reasoning, ensuring consistent responses across all tool interactions. Comprehensive testing confirms that all scenarios now return successful results.

items.title: Improve error messaging for video failures in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update enhances the error messaging for failed video tasks in the cqtai module. It now includes the upstream error message in the response, allowing users to understand the specific reason for the failure instead of receiving a generic 'unknown error' message. This change applies to both Veo and Sora2 tasks, improving clarity and reducing the need for manual log investigations.

items.title: Refactor billing to server-side for efficiency in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update moves the billing process from the worker pod to a dedicated server-side monitor, enhancing efficiency and eliminating the need for a gateway in the worker pod. Key changes include modifications to server.py and main.py, ensuring that the worker only interacts with MongoDB. The billing semantics remain unchanged, ensuring a smooth transition without impacting existing consumer contracts.

items.title: Fix voice connection and UI issues in Nexior 🎤
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues with voice calls in the Nexior application. The voice picker has been relocated to avoid interference with the wallet display, and a new WebSocket query parameter for voice has been implemented to resolve call errors. Additionally, mid-call voice changes are now handled more safely to prevent disruptions.

items.title: Fix voice parameter handling for realtime calls in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where realtime voice calls failed due to a missing 'session.type' parameter. The fix includes validating the voice parameter from the query string and ensuring it is correctly nested in the session update. Additionally, the session sanitizer now injects the 'session.type' to maintain compatibility with the GA structure, ensuring all updates are valid.

items.title: Fix tool-continuation issues in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses multiple causes of 400 errors in aichat2 conversations, specifically related to assistant message prefill and DeepSeek reasoning content. Key fixes include ensuring that trailing prose is correctly folded into assistant messages and persisting reasoning content for DeepSeek models. These changes enhance the reliability of tool-continuation interactions and improve overall user experience.

items.title: Desktop Client Implementation Status and Next Steps in Index 🚀
items.summary: The README for the Nexior desktop client has been updated to reflect the completed implementation and merged changes. Key updates include a detailed implementation status table and a list of remaining tasks such as CI certs, beta rehearsals, and design verifications. This progress enhances the overall functionality and readiness of the desktop application for users.

items.title: Enhance Native Redirect Flow for Desktop in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a centralized native deep-link flow for the Nexior desktop client, ensuring secure OAuth callbacks by echoing the `state` parameter. Key changes include the addition of a new utility for managing deep links and updates to several Vue components to utilize this functionality. The implementation maintains backward compatibility for mobile users while enhancing security against potential phishing attacks.

items.title: Fix voice call issues and enhance wallet selection in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update addresses four key bugs in the Realtime voice-call screen, including fixing the orb clipping issue in Safari, resolving a stale cancel error, and improving the wallet app selection process. A new voice picker has also been added, allowing users to select their preferred voice during calls. These changes enhance the overall user experience and functionality of the application.

items.title: Enhance Automatic Adversarial Review for All Changes in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update makes the automatic adversarial review unconditional, ensuring it runs before every PR and plan finalization without exceptions. Key documentation files have been updated to reflect this change, enhancing clarity and usability for authors. The new process is efficient, allowing trivial changes to self-converge quickly, thus minimizing overhead.

items.title: Upgrade actions/checkout to v7 in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the actions/checkout dependency from version 4 to version 7 across multiple workflow files. Key changes include improved handling for fork PRs and various dependency updates, enhancing overall functionality and security. This upgrade ensures better compatibility and performance for CI/CD processes.

items.title: Revamping Digital Human to Pull Mode in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the digital-human system from a push model to a pull model, integrating it with the maestro on a unified GPU queue. Key additions include a new MongoDB-backed task store, a FastAPI cloud server, and a dedicated pull worker for GPU tasks. The changes are additive and dormant, ensuring no disruption to existing production behavior while enhancing future scalability.

items.title: Transform CLAUDE.md into a Real File for Better Compatibility in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the symlink of `CLAUDE.md` with a regular file that imports `@AGENTS.md`, ensuring cross-platform compatibility. The change eliminates issues with Windows checkouts where symlinks are not supported, preserving the single source of truth in `AGENTS.md`. This adjustment enhances accessibility and maintains the integrity of project documentation.

items.title: Improve Error Classification for Task Creation in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the error handling for the `createTask` function in the cqtai module. It now correctly classifies upstream errors, reducing false 5xx alerts by mapping specific error codes to appropriate HTTP responses. The changes enhance clarity for clients and improve logging for better traceability.

items.title: Enhance Maestro with GPU Lock and Watchdog in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates GPU-lock functionality for the maestro worker, ensuring exclusive access to a shared GPU box while preventing double occupancy. A standalone watchdog has been added to manage stuck jobs, and deployment configurations have been updated for seamless operation. The changes maintain existing cloud behavior while enabling enhanced GPU capabilities on local setups.

items.title: Enhance Maestro with Owner-Fence and Billing Improvements in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates owner-fence concurrency control and a poison budget mechanism into the live maestro worker, ensuring that stale workers do not execute late writes. Additionally, it implements owner-guarded billing to prevent double charges, enhancing the reliability of the billing process. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate these changes, ensuring a safer rollout.

items.title: Streamlined Desktop Release Pipeline in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR finalizes the desktop release pipeline for Nexior, incorporating build, sign, notarize, and publish processes for both Windows and macOS. Key updates include the addition of E2E smoke tests, automated artifact uploads to Tencent Cloud COS, and enhanced documentation for release procedures. The workflow now requires admin approval before any release, ensuring a robust deployment process.

items.title: Exciting Electron Shell Features Added in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces significant enhancements to the Nexior desktop client, focusing on the Electron main process. Key updates include a custom protocol for serving the SPA, OAuth deep-link handling, and an in-memory state nonce store for secure authentication. Additionally, the auto-updater has been integrated, ensuring seamless updates for users.

items.title: Launch of GPU-queue Foundation Library in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the foundational GPU-queue library under `maestro/worker/app/queue/`, featuring a schema-agnostic Mongo queue, GPU mutual exclusion, and a watchdog for stuck processes. Comprehensive unit tests have been added to ensure reliability, and CI has been updated to include Python testing for the new components. The changes are additive, ensuring no disruption to existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Desktop Client Authentication Flow in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a surface-aware authentication flow for the Nexior desktop client, ensuring that login functionality works seamlessly without affecting web or native behaviors. Key changes include the extraction of SSO code exchange logic, updates to the authentication panel, and the addition of localized error messages for login link expiration across 18 languages. The changes have been verified with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Kickstarting the Electron Desktop Client in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR lays the groundwork for the Nexior desktop client by introducing a dedicated surface for desktop functionality. Key additions include constants for desktop, updated utility functions to recognize the desktop environment, and a new bridge for Electron integration. All changes are additive, ensuring no impact on existing web, Android, or iOS functionalities.

items.title: Unifying GPU-Queue Architecture for Enhanced Efficiency in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a unified GPU-queue architecture that consolidates multiple projects, including maestro and digital-human, onto a single GPU. Key changes include the implementation of per-project Mongo queues, a robust kernel flock for GPU locking, and a streamlined server/worker model to maximize GPU utilization while preventing out-of-memory errors. The new design aims to enhance scalability and maintain independent project deployments.

items.title: Exciting Plans for Nexior Desktop Clients in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update outlines the plan for developing Windows and macOS desktop clients for Nexior using Electron. Key decisions include a full signed and notarized release with auto-update capabilities, and adjustments to the existing web build to accommodate desktop-specific features. The effort is estimated to take approximately two weeks for a complete release, focusing on ensuring a smooth authentication flow and compliance with platform requirements.

items.title: Boost AGENT_TIMEOUT to 90min for heavy workflows in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the `MAESTRO_AGENT_TIMEOUT` from 40 to 90 minutes to accommodate heavy workflows that were timing out during final assembly. Additionally, the `STALE_CLAIM_SECONDS` now derives from the `AGENT_TIMEOUT`, ensuring that stale claims exceed the timeout duration. This change is crucial for preventing finish-line timeouts while further throughput improvements are planned.

items.title: Enhance glow reduction for digital humans in Platform Service ✨
items.summary: This update introduces a stable glow reduction technique for digital humans, eliminating flicker during video processing. The new patch adjusts the per-channel mean within the face mask to match the original image, ensuring temporal stability. Additionally, the guidance scale has been lowered from 2.0 to 1.5, which helps maintain the mouth's appearance closer to the source while reducing the glow effect.
